content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed the full diff across 13 files implementing: (a) the `explicit_id` flag on `BeadProposal` with backward-compatible shape signature computation, (b) live/audit workspace split moving mutable state to `.git/ralph-burning-live` with mirroring to `.ralph-burning`, and (c) updated bead resolution logic in `resolve_bead_plan`. Tests are comprehensive and all pass. Found two substantive issues: a backward compatibility gap in bead resolution for pre-upgrade plan.json files, and a missing audit mirror in the drift detection write path.

## Amendments

1. In `src/cli/project.rs`, `resolve_bead_plan` breaks for legacy plan.json files written before this change. Legacy canonicalized plan.json has `bead_id: Some("milestone.bead-N")` but no `explicit_id` field (deserializes to `None`). The new `proposal_matches_bead_id` returns false because `None != Some(true)`. The new `proposal_is_title_fallback_candidate` also returns false because `(None.is_none() && Some(...).is_none())` is false AND `None == Some(false)` is false. Neither resolution path matches, silently degrading to `membership_confirmed: false` (losing per-bead flow_override and plan version validation). Fix: in `proposal_is_title_fallback_candidate`, treat `explicit_id: None` with `bead_id: Some(canonical_implicit_id)` as a title-fallback candidate — e.g. add a branch: `proposal.explicit_id.is_none() && proposal.bead_id.as_deref().is_some_and(|c| implicit_slot_matches(c, milestone_id, implicit_bead_id))`.
   Rationale: Users upgrading the binary with existing milestones (plan.json on disk without explicit_id) will silently lose per-bead flow overrides and plan metadata validation when creating projects from beads. The degradation is silent (no error), which makes it hard to diagnose.
2. In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/drift.rs:237-260`, `sync_project_prompt_hash` writes the updated project.toml to `FileSystem::project_root()` (which resolves to the live workspace) but does not mirror the change to the audit workspace. Every other write path in this diff (`write_project_policy_config`, `write_run_snapshot`, `append_journal_line`, `write_payload_artifact`, `write_amendment`, `create_project`, prompt update/rollback) mirrors to the audit root. This omission means the audit `project.toml` will have a stale `prompt_hash` after drift sync. Add `FileSystem::mirror_project_file(base_dir, project_id, PROJECT_CONFIG_FILE, &updated);` after the `write_atomic` call.
   Rationale: The audit workspace is designed to be a reviewable mirror of durable project state. A stale prompt_hash in the audit copy breaks the invariant that every durable write is mirrored, and could cause incorrect drift detection if the system ever falls back to reading from the audit directory (e.g. after live workspace deletion and re-seed).
